_help.about: HexManager Hexpansion Type Definitions - edit this file to add your own hexpansion types.
instructions: Add your hexpansion to the 'hexpansions' list below.
vid and pid values may be written as quoted hex strings (e.g. "0xCAFE"). Quoted hex strings must use speech marks to be valid JSON.
Only 'pid' and 'name' are required; all other fields are optional and have sensible defaults.
The app .mpy file must be placed in the HexManager EEPROM folder on the badge.
Not sure what an .mpy file is? .mpy files are compiled from .py source using the mpy-cross utility (see README.md).

fields.name: REQUIRED. Human-friendly display name shown in HexManager, e.g. 'HexDrive'.
fields.friendly_name: OPTIONAL. Short name written into the EEPROM header and shown by BadgeOS insertion notifications. Maximum 9 chars. Defaults to 'name'.
fields.sub_type: OPTIONAL. Short label for this specific variant, e.g. '2 Motor'. Shown to the user alongside the name.
fields.vid: OPTIONAL. Vendor ID (16-bit integer), e.g. "0xCAFE". Default 0xCAFE which is the UHB-IF uncontrolled VID.
fields.eeprom_total_size: OPTIONAL. Total EEPROM size in BYTES.
fields.eeprom_page_size: OPTIONAL. EEPROM page size in BYTES.
fields.app_mpy_name: OPTIONAL. Filename (without .mpy extension) of the compiled app to write to the hexpansion EEPROM. File must be in the EEPROM sub-folder of the HexManager app on the badge (e.g. /apps/TeamRobotmad_HexManager/EEPROM/myhex.mpy). NOTE: the file is renamed to 'app.mpy' when programmed onto the EEPROM so that Badge OS automatically detects and runs it when the hexpansion is inserted into a badge slot.
fields.app_mpy_version: OPTIONAL. Version number (integer or string) of the .mpy app. Used to detect when an upgrade is needed.
fields.app_name: OPTIONAL. Python class name of the hexpansion app, e.g. 'MyHexApp'. Used to check if the app is running.

hexpansions._comment: Flopagon - small 2048-byte EEPROM. EEPROM too small for the app.
hexpansions.name: Flopagon
